description: Add property tests asserting commutativity, associativity, and idempotence for CanonicalState and StoreState joins using the generic CRDT law harness.
title: Extend CRDT law tests to CanonicalState and StoreState

acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Migration integration coverage exercises retryable push-race exhaustion and asserts the terminal error path.
- [ ] Migration integration coverage asserts backup-ref pruning behavior under pressure and protects the current target backup.
- [ ] The new tests fail if retry-budget handling or backup pruning regresses.
description:
**Problem**
The migration CLI suite proves successful retry recovery and backup-ref creation, but it still does not pin two safety contracts from the hard-cutover plan: terminal behavior after exhausting retryable push races, and backup-ref retention/pruning behavior when backup pressure exceeds the cap. Lower-level sync tests cover pieces of this, but the end-to-end migration layer would not catch a regression in terminal retry handling or backup pruning.

**Files:** `crates/beads-rs/tests/integration/cli/migration.rs`
design:
**Design**
Add migration CLI integration coverage for the missing safety paths.

- Force repeated retryable push rejection until the migration retry budget is exhausted, and assert the final user-visible error.
- Create backup-ref pressure through migration rewrite paths and assert pruning-to-cap behavior while preserving the current target backup safety.
- Reuse the existing migration fixture helpers and test hooks so the cases stay deterministic.

notes.author: darin@book
notes.content: Final audit on 2026-03-11 against /Users/darin/src/personal/beads-rs-legacy-deps-v1-cutover @ 015888b8: the migration integration suite now pins both remaining backup/retry safety contracts from the hard-cutover plan. Coverage now exercises retry-budget exhaustion with the terminal TooManyRetries path and asserts that migration-triggered backup publication prunes back to the cap while preserving the protected backup for the pre-rewrite local store head. Verification: cargo fmt --all --check; just dylint; cargo clippy --all-features -- -D warnings; cargo test; cargo test -p beads-rs migration --features slow-tests -- --nocapture. --repo /Users/darin/src/personal/beads-rs

title: Migration CLI coverage should pin retry-limit and backup-pruning safety

acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] `WireBeadFull` cannot represent conflicting workflow/claim timestamps.
- [ ] Import/export preserves canonical stamps without redundancy.
- [ ] Backward compatibility path exists or explicit migration is documented.
- [ ] Tests cover at least one inconsistent legacy payload being rejected or normalized.
description:
**Problem**
`WireBeadFull` includes redundant workflow/claim timestamps (`closed_at/closed_by`, `assignee_at`) that can conflict with the canonical LWW stamps derived from `workflow`/`claim`. Conversions ignore these fields, which means the wire type can represent contradictory states. This violates “information holds its shape” and creates a lying API for checkpoints/snapshots.

Key references:
- `crates/beads-core/src/wire_bead.rs:303` — `WireBeadFull` fields.
- `crates/beads-core/src/composite.rs:99` — closure/claim timestamps are derived from LWW stamps.

Severity: snapshot import/export can silently accept inconsistent data; types do not encode invariants.
design:
**Design**
Make workflow/claim snapshot representations canonical and non‑redundant.

Option A (sum types):
- Replace `status + closed_*` with `WorkflowSnapshot`:
  - `Open {}`
  - `InProgress {}`
  - `Closed { at: WireStamp, by: ActorId, reason: Option<String>, on_branch: Option<String> }`
- Replace `assignee + assignee_at + assignee_expires` with `ClaimSnapshot`:
  - `Unclaimed {}`
  - `Claimed { assignee: ActorId, at: WireStamp, expires: Option<WallClock> }`

Option B (derived-only):
- Remove `closed_at/closed_by` and `assignee_at` from `WireBeadFull`; derive from stamps only.

Given compatibility needs, Option A keeps wire expressiveness while encoding invariants.

Migration:
- Adjust serde for backward compatibility (accept old fields, emit new shape).
- Update `WireBeadFull::from_view` and `From<WireBeadFull> for Bead` accordingly.
title: Encode workflow/claim snapshot invariants in WireBeadFull

acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Repo/runtime/data-dir setup is shared instead of copied across CLI/daemon integration suites
- [ ] Existing tests preserve isolation semantics while using the common helpers
- [ ] Slow-path tests stop rebuilding equivalent scaffolding ad hoc
description:
**Problem**
`crates/beads-rs/tests/integration/cli/critical_path.rs` and `crates/beads-rs/tests/integration/cli/migration.rs` both own near-identical temp repo/runtime/data-dir setup and command bootstrapping. Similar assembly-owned setup logic is also repeated across daemon integration fixtures. This increases startup cost and makes isolation fixes land in multiple places.

**Design**
Extract shared assembly-owned helpers for repo/runtime/data-dir setup, command construction, and daemon lifecycle into the existing `integration/fixtures/` surface. Move duplicate setup out of individual test files and keep ownership boundaries consistent with `test_design.md`.

**Files**
- `crates/beads-rs/tests/integration/fixtures/mod.rs`
- `crates/beads-rs/tests/integration/fixtures/*.rs`
- `crates/beads-rs/tests/integration/cli/critical_path.rs`
- `crates/beads-rs/tests/integration/cli/migration.rs`
- `crates/beads-rs/tests/integration/daemon/*.rs`

notes.author: darin@book
notes.content:
This bead has real progress but is not closed yet.

Landed so far:
- shared `BdRuntimeRepo` / `bd_runtime` command setup and daemon bootstrap helpers
- shared wait/process helpers in `fixtures/wait.rs`
- centralized daemon socket/meta/store discovery for CLI + daemon integration tests
- adopters in `critical_path`, `migration`, `admin`, `lifecycle`, `crash_recovery`, `realtime`, and related fixtures

Remaining duplication still worth another slice:
- `repl_rig` status polling and retry loops
- `daemon/logging.rs` still owns a one-off runtime fixture
- migration store-ref setup still has local-only fixture logic

Audit receipts backing that call came from the current subagent read-through and the current green fast/slow stack.

notes.author: darin@book
notes.content:
Current consolidation slice landed under `beads-rs-iymc` and further reduced local test harness ownership without creating new one-off setup code.

What changed:
- added shared `fixtures::temp` for short integration temp roots and socket-path assertions
- moved `repl_rig`, `daemon/logging`, and the tailnet trace harness onto the shared temp fixture surface
- documented the shared temp-root rule in `AGENTS.md` so new tests do not regress back to `current_dir()/tmp`

Fresh receipts from `/Users/darin/src/personal/beads-rs`:
- `cargo test -p beads-rs --test integration fixtures::temp::tests -- --nocapture`
- `cargo test -p beads-rs --test integration daemon::logging --features slow-tests -- --nocapture`
- `cargo nextest run --profile slow -p beads-rs --test integration --all-features --features slow-tests daemon::repl_e2e:: --status-level none --final-status-level slow` -> `13.013s`

This bead stays open because the migration store-ref setup still owns too much local fixture logic inside `cli/migration.rs`.

title: Deduplicate assembly integration harness setup across CLI and daemon tests
